metadata.dataset_name: Domain Concept Encyclopedia with Multi-Modal Inputs
metadata.description: Core concepts across domains with their typical multi-modal capture formats and cross-domain connection potential

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Decomposing motion into independent horizontal/vertical components
common_misconceptions: Horizontal motion affects vertical fall time

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Total quantity conserved while transforming between forms
Reducing moment of inertia increases angular velocity
common_misconceptions: Angular momentum is the same as linear momentum

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Observation method determines which properties manifest
Systems naturally move toward disorder without energy input
Force = mass × acceleration; action-reaction pairs
common_misconceptions: Force is required to maintain motion

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Changing magnetic field induces electric current
Matching driving frequency to natural frequency amplifies response
Self-similar decomposition with base case termination
Systematic exploration strategy matched to problem structure
Store frequently-accessed items in fast, nearby storage
Distribute load to prevent any single node from being overwhelmed
common_misconceptions: Load balancing eliminates the need for scaling

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Mathematical transformation making information accessible only to authorized parties
Standardized contract enabling interoperability between systems
common_misconceptions: APIs are only for tech companies
APIs are the same as databases

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Fail fast and isolate to prevent cascading failures
common_misconceptions: Circuit breakers are only for electrical systems

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Store complete history of events to reconstruct state and enable audit
common_misconceptions: Event sourcing is the same as event-driven
Event sourcing is too complex for all systems

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Constant rate of increase produces accelerating growth
common_misconceptions: Doubling time is constant in percentage terms

concepts.key_transferable_principle: No player benefits by unilaterally changing strategy at equilibrium
common_misconceptions: Nash equilibrium is always optimal for all

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Update belief incrementally as new evidence arrives
common_misconceptions: Prior doesn't matter with enough data

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Value increases super-linearly with number of participants
common_misconceptions: All networks have strong network effects

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Small fraction produces majority of outcomes
Output fed back as input amplifies (positive) or stabilizes (negative) system
Choose first option meeting threshold rather than seeking optimal
Asymmetric payoff: limited downside, unlimited upside
Differential survival and reproduction of variants best adapted to environment
common_misconceptions: Survival of the fittest means strongest

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Self-regulating maintenance of stable internal state through negative feedback
Distributed resource-sharing networks create mutual benefit and resilience
Systems that gain from shocks and volatility
Decentralized agents following simple local rules produce complex global behavior
Environment affects gene expression without changing DNA sequence
common_misconceptions: Lamarck was right about acquired traits

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Mental discomfort from holding contradictory beliefs drives belief/behavior change
common_misconceptions: People always change beliefs to match behavior

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Complete absorption when challenge matches skill level
common_misconceptions: Flow is the same as relaxation

concepts.key_transferable_principle: First piece of information disproportionately influences subsequent judgments
Humans disproportionately value immediate over future rewards
common_misconceptions: Everyone discounts at the same rate

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Awareness and control of one's own cognitive processes
common_misconceptions: Metacognition is the same as intelligence

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Learned association between neutral stimulus and reflexive response
Seeking confirming evidence while ignoring disconfirming evidence
common_misconceptions: Smart people don't have confirmation bias

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Losses feel ~2x as painful as equivalent gains feel good
common_misconceptions: Loss aversion applies to everyone equally

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Self-reinforcing loop where each iteration makes the next easier
common_misconceptions: Any positive loop is a flywheel

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Value to each user increases with total number of users
common_misconceptions: All products benefit from network effects

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Sustainable barrier protecting against competition
Past unrecoverable costs should not influence future decisions
common_misconceptions: Considering past investment is always wrong
Sunk costs are only about money

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Adoption follows predictable S-curve through distinct psychographic segments
common_misconceptions: Innovators are the most profitable segment

concepts.key_transferable_principle: When a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure
common_misconceptions: Goodhart's law means all metrics are bad
The problem is the metric, not the target

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Work expands to fill the time available for its completion
Deconstruct problems to fundamental truths and build up from there
common_misconceptions: It's the same as root cause analysis

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Solve backward from failure rather than forward from success
common_misconceptions: Only works for avoiding bad outcomes

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Consider consequences of consequences
common_misconceptions: More orders of thinking are always better

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Understand purpose of existing structures before changing them
common_misconceptions: Understanding is impossible so change is always risky

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Decision-makers should bear consequences of their decisions
common_misconceptions: Skin in game means high investment only
No skin in game is always bad

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Models are simplifications, not the reality they represent
Complex system-level behaviors arise from simple local interactions
common_misconceptions: Emergent properties can be predicted from components

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Limited downside, unlimited upside creates favorable expected value
Non-perishable things that survived X years will survive another X years
Faster decision cycles create competitive advantage
Imagine failure and work backward to identify preventable causes
Assign someone to argue against the group's position to find weaknesses
Error signal flows backward to adjust all contributing parameters
Focus processing on most relevant parts of input
Knowledge from one task accelerates learning on related tasks
Every transaction recorded as equal and opposite entries
common_misconceptions: Debits are bad, credits are good

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Learning through guided questioning rather than direct instruction
Approximate unknown quantities using known quantities and logical decomposition
Build in buffer for inevitable uncertainty and error
common_misconceptions: Margin of safety is wasted capacity

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Multiple systems performing same function prevent single-point failure
Test assumptions quickly with minimal investment
common_misconceptions: Fail fast means give up quickly

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Successful outliers in same resource-constrained environment reveal actionable solutions
Separate decision quality from outcome quality; think probabilistically
Choose to minimize regret at end of life, not maximize short-term comfort
Prepare all resources before execution begins
Among competing hypotheses, prefer the one with fewest assumptions
State depends on history, not just current conditions; path matters
Threshold where self-sustaining process begins
common_misconceptions: Critical mass is a fixed number

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Small continuous changes produce sudden discontinuous state change
Operate only where you have genuine expertise
Improve internal structure without changing external behavior
common_misconceptions: Only needed when something is broken

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Creative genius emerges from collaborative scene, not isolated individuals
Knowing how is different from knowing that; different neural systems
common_misconceptions: Reading about it is the same as doing it
Experts can easily explain their skills

concepts.key_transferable_principle: Distinguish meaningful pattern from random variation
Limitations force novel solutions that abundance wouldn't discover
